extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won stage 1 Tour Down Under after Deceuninck-QuickStep delivered him at 200m, holding off PHILIPSEN's superior acceleration with sustained power.
tactical_patterns: LAWLESS Chris positioned EF for sprint suggesting HOWSON not pure GC focus despite hilly profile tomorrow.
Deceuninck-QuickStep with MØRKØV delivered BENNETT at 200m, unusually early for modern leadouts, exploiting BENNETT's sustained power advantage.
GC riders like PORTE stayed forward through final lap indicating positioning critical even on non-selective sprint stages.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: power-based sprint style holds acceleration better than PHILIPSEN late, suits earlier leadout deployment
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: negative: superior acceleration faded final 100m, suggests immature power endurance for extended sprints
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: positive: contesting intermediate sprint at km15 signals GC aggression unusual for secondary TDU favorite
rider_signals.ROSSKOPF Joey: neutral: polka dot hunting suggests CCC lacking sprint or GC options stage 1
rider_signals.VAN BAARLE Dylan: negative: Ineos sprinting with BASKAK indicates VIVIANI already compromised or team confusion
